title: vilfa marginata
intro: Vilfa marginata, also known as Sweet Woodruff or Woodruff, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Its medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, while also acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Vilfa marginata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, and is also used in potpourri and perfumes for its sweet, vanilla-like fragrance.
